reasoning: The market is predicting that the Fed will enact at most 1 or 2 cuts in the entirety of 2026, mapping to a median dot plot reduction of only ~20-25 bps for the year (from ~3.6% at the end of 2025 down to 3.4% at the end of 2026). With 8 FOMC meetings in 2026 and only 1-2 expected cuts, the base probability of a 'no change' (hold) at any given meeting is quite high. Even if cuts are skewed toward the Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) meetings (March, June, September, December), a single assumed cut would imply roughly a 25-35% chance of occurring specifically in March, leaving a 65-75% probability of a hold. While institutions like Goldman Sachs explicitly forecast a cut in March 2026, the general pace of easing is expected to slow significantly by early 2026 as rates approach the terminal range. Since 'no change' is the default for most meetings in a slow-cutting or stable environment, it represents the most likely outcome for March 2026.
